failure_modes.text: Repeated content can increase cognitive load when the duplication adds no real protection or clarification.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit each redundant element for marginal value: if duplication adds no new protection, clarity, or resilience, remove it to reduce cognitive load.

failure_modes.text: Teams can keep accumulating backup analyses or perspectives until the work stalls into analysis paralysis.
failure_modes.source_quote: An over-reliance on seeking redundant analyses or perspectives can lead to getting mired in analysis paralysis
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a backup threshold: define how many alternative analyses or perspectives are needed, and stop accumulating once the diminishing-returns point is reached.

failure_modes.text: A system can still be fragile if planners assume a conjunctive chain will work without enough redundancy in risk assessment.
failure_modes.source_quote: When planning, people overestimate the overall probability of success for complex conjunctive events
failure_modes.mitigation: Check conjunctive chains for single points of failure: if the plan requires too many sequential successes, add redundancy at the weakest link rather than spreading it evenly.

premortem_questions.text: What exact failure mode justifies this extra layer, copy, or backup path?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger When Duplication Is Added Without a Clear Failure Mode

premortem_questions.text: Would anything important be lost if this duplicate element were removed?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this was cut, would anything be lost?

premortem_questions.text: Are we adding redundancy that changes survival or only adding drag that feels safer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when repetition or duplication increases robustness more than it increases drag

heuristics.text: Add redundancy only when a named failure mode makes the backup path materially valuable.
Use repeated review or rehearsal to strengthen retention, not to create framework soup.
heuristics.source_quote: reviewing information multiple times strengthens neural connections

heuristics.text: Trim duplicate content aggressively when it increases cognitive load more than resilience.
heuristics.source_quote: presenting information that is simultaneously available in another format is harmful

curation_notes.summary: Focused on strategic duplication that changes resilience or retention rather than on redundancy as generic extra effort.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
